target.title: fix: keep yielded parent runs deferred until subagents settle
target.body:
## Summary

This change promotes yielded-parent semantics into a shared parent lifecycle classifier and routes the affected cron, subagent-registry, and Discord/source-message paths through that state instead of letting each consumer infer completion from terminal-shaped model-attempt events, missing final text, or abort-shaped control flow.

The shipped behavior is:

- yielded parent + active descendants is treated as waiting/deferred, not ordinary completion;
- yielded parent + no descendant is treated as diagnostic error;
- cron delivery waits for a real final outcome instead of counting the yielded attempt as `ok / not-delivered`;
- Discord/source-message progress remains parent-owned while the yielded parent is waiting;
- descendant settlement wakes/reconciles yielded parents through the existing continuation paths.

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where parent-agent runs that spawned child sessions/subagents and then called `sessions_yield` could look finished before the parent task actually completed.

The most visible failures were:

- isolated cron `agentTurn` jobs with valid delivery targets could finish as `ok / not-delivered` because the yielded parent had no final payload yet;
- Discord source-message lifecycle could clear parent progress/done indicators while child work was still active or while the parent was waiting to reconcile final output;
- run history/status surfaces made yielded parent work look like delivery failure, ordinary success with no output, or completed source-message work instead of a paused/resumable parent lifecycle.

`session_yield` is supposed to end the current model turn, not the parent task. This PR makes that distinction explicit for the affected runtime consumers.

## User Impact / Intended Outcome

Operators and developers can now rely on `sessions_yield` after spawning child/subagent work without the parent run being misrepresented as complete at the cron or Discord lifecycle boundary.

Practically:

- autonomous development crons stop inflating misleading `ok / not-delivered` results for intentionally yielded runs;
- cron history exposes a distinct `deferred` state for yielded parent handoff;
- Discord progress no longer marks the parent source message done just because the parent yielded while children continue;
- yielded parent runs are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles;
- debugging points to the parent/child lifecycle state instead of falsely implying delivery target resolution failed.

## Linked Context

- #95535 — `sessions_yield` isolated cron resumption/delivery gap.
- #92369 — subagent orchestration in isolated cron sessions needs reliable spawn/wait/aggregate semantics.
- #92116 — `sessions_yield` completion delivered as passive context can break orchestration loops.
- #86537 — subagent terminal reconciliation can expire suspended final delivery.
- #86538 — session write-lock timeouts can block subagent delivery lanes.
- #95833 — subagent abort-settle/session-lock failure can break session continuation.
- #92460 — isolated cron completion delivery gap around final controller return.
- #95604 — Discord subagent progress visibility.
- #94153 — durable queued subagent completion announcements.
- #95847 — requester-consumed descendant completion accounting.

## What Changes

### Shared parent lifecycle state

- Adds `src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.ts`.
- Classifies lifecycle events with explicit yielded/waiting vs terminal semantics.
- Exports the classifier through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/reply-runtime`.
- Routes subagent registry and Discord source-message handling through the classifier instead of local `yielded && paused` inference.

### Cron lifecycle and delivery

- Adds first-class cron run status `deferred`.
- Classifies yielded descendant handoff before delivery-mode branching, so text, structured payloads, `delivery.mode: none`, source-satisfied delivery, and normal direct delivery all share the same semantics.
- Prevents duplicate/interim parent announcements while descendants are active.
- Records yielded-without-descendant as explicit error diagnostic.
- Keeps deferred manual task runs active rather than failing them.
- Disables one-shot deferred cron jobs after handoff to avoid retry loops.

### Persistence, protocol, CLI, and UI

- Extends run-log JSON/SQLite encoding and status filtering for `deferred`.
- Extends Gateway protocol schemas and cron server filters.
- Extends CLI status/wait typing and docs.
- Extends hook-visible status typing.
- Adds Control UI status/filter labels and generated locale fallbacks.

### Parent/subagent reconciliation

- Tracks yielded/paused parents that still have pending descendants.
- Marks yielded parents with `wakeOnDescendantSettle` so they are resumed/reconciled when descendant work settles.
- Handles child success/failure settlement through existing subagent lifecycle recovery paths.
- Adds regression coverage for yielded parents being reconciled after child failure so diagnostics can wake the parent.

### Discord/source-message lifecycle

- Projects lifecycle events from agent execution into reply/channel handling.
- Uses the shared classifier to identify yielded/waiting parent state.
- Preserves in-progress/progress representation for yielded parents with active children.
- Avoids prematurely marking Discord status reactions done or clearing draft/progress state when the parent yielded and remains waiting.

### Concurrent user-message semantics

`docs/tools/subagents.md` documents the selected policy: if a human sends another message while a requester is yielded, the new message starts a normal turn for the same requester session. OpenClaw keeps the yielded run visible as waiting on descendant completion rather than converting it into a stale final reply; when descendant completion arrives, the completion event is handed back to the requester for synthesis.

## Implementation Notes

Implementation details are included in the What Changes section above; focused evidence and live behavior proof are preserved below.

## Tests / Verification

Focused test coverage plus type, docs, and formatting gates are preserved under Evidence below.

## Evidence

### Rebase/Squash/Proof

Current rebased/squashed PR head: `f0a18c53ea8c2b86eb06e4e226d7db85d4d2154f`.

Latest pushed commit after same-base rebase and squash: `a8aa8bfb134e2b7d5ce77510dc076c13dfd0e4a0`.

All six coordinated PR branches in this batch were rebased onto the same pinned upstream base commit:

```text
dc9c11be917ebdc711b956250aa80a8e5b47bea6
```

Post-rebase local verification included conflict-marker/diff hygiene plus focused branch-specific gates. Final proof marker for this PR:

```text
POST_REBASE_20260623_YIELD_LIFECYCLE_SDK_SURFACE_REMEDIATED_OK
```

- Base: `654544b6b7c49d78afbacacd4861bcc830172780`
- Head after refresh: `31d2476f773adc571c95196e4d51ff81a3215265`
- Conflict resolution: Resolved `scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` budget conflict by keeping the PR-added SDK surface counts on top of current `main`; squashed the two rebased commits into one coherent commit.
- Validation: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-cache-pr95996 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.test.ts src/agents/sessions-spawn-hooks.test.ts src/cron/service/ops.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts` -> passed, 4 files / 119 tests across 3 shards.
- Follow-up settlement validation after the requester-consumed accounting refresh: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts -- -t 'yield|settle|descendant'` -> passed, 6 selected tests / 59 skipped.
- Push: force-with-lease to `kklouzal/openclaw:p1/sessions-yield-cron-lifecycle` completed.

### Real / Live Behavior Proof

Runtime: Dev-01 installed stack commit `5c1fd341c89b1493fed1ea6014388394291450c3` via `/home/kklouzal/update.sh`; `openclaw --version` reported `OpenClaw 2026.6.9 (5c1fd34)` and `openclaw-gateway.service` was active from that stack.

Focused live cron/subagent handoff run:

```bash
openclaw cron add --json \
  --name pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z \
  --at +6h --keep-after-run --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --tools sessions_spawn,sessions_yield --timeout-seconds 600 \
  --message 'Live proof marker pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z ...'

openclaw cron run 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 \
  --wait --wait-timeout 12m --poll-interval 5s --timeout 600000
```

Redacted observed output from the live run:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "enqueued": true,
  "runId": "manual:514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938:1782253518340:1",
  "completed": true,
  "status": "deferred",
  "run": {
    "status": "deferred",
    "diagnostics": {
      "summary": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active",
      "entries": [
        {
          "source": "delivery",
          "severity": "warn",
          "message": "cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active"
        }
      ]
    },
    "deliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "delivery": { "fallbackUsed": false, "delivered": false },
    "sessionKey": "agent:main:cron:<job-id>:run:<session-id>"
  }
}
cron_run_exit=1
```

The non-zero wait exit is expected for this proof: `openclaw cron run --wait` exits non-zero for persisted `deferred` status, while still returning the run record. Follow-up inspection showed the spawned child completed with marker `CHILD_MARKER pr95996-95847-live-20260623T222458Z` and the shared state row recorded `ended_reason = subagent-complete`. The cron shared-state row for the same job persisted `last_run_status = deferred`, `last_delivery_status = not-requested`, and diagnostic summary `cron isolated agent yielded while descendant runs are still active`.

Proof sources:

- CLI run output captured in the maintainer live shell on Dev-01.
- Cron run log: `openclaw cron runs --id 514fd6e1-862e-43ca-87e3-da1197a7c938 --limit 5`.
- Shared state DB checks scoped to that job/run in `/home/kklouzal/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
- Child/session redacted traces scoped to `agent:main:cron:<job-id>:run:<session-id>` and `agent:main:subagent:<child-session-id>`.

Remaining proof gap: this live proof covers the cron yielded-parent/subagent deferred lifecycle path. It does not provide Discord visual status-reaction proof.

### Focused test coverage

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/agent-lifecycle-parent-state.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-queries.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts \
  ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts \
  src/cron/service/ops.test.ts \
  src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts \
  src/cron/run-log.test.ts \
  packages/gateway-protocol/src/cron-validators.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
```

Passed.

Coverage includes:

- shared parent lifecycle classifier behavior;
- yielded cron parent + active descendants -> `deferred`;
- yielded cron parent + no descendant -> diagnostic `error`;
- structured/no-delivery/source-satisfied delivery paths classified before delivery;
- no duplicate parent interim delivery while descendants are active;
- deferred run-log persistence and filtering;
- deferred Gateway protocol validators;
- manual `cron.run` deferred task-ledger behavior;
- one-shot deferred cron disabling;
- Control UI deferred status/filter rendering;
- CLI wait/status behavior for deferred;
- yielded parent descendant-settle wake/reconciliation;
- yielded parent child-failure diagnostics;
- Discord yielded-parent progress preservation;
- plugin SDK subpath export coverage for the classifier.

### Type, docs, and formatting gates

```bash
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src --noEmit
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
pnpm tsgo:test:packages --noEmit
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/tools/subagents.md
git diff --check
```

Passed.

Final local gate marker:

```text
FINAL_PR_READY_YIELD_LIFECYCLE_GATES_OK
```

## Compatibility and Risk

Compatibility and risk are addressed by preserving existing completion/delivery semantics while routing yielded-parent cases through the shared parent lifecycle state described above.

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown

Source: 63 files, +470 / -111
Tests: 10 files, +401 / -15
Total: 73 files, +871 / -126

## Reviewer Checklist

- Review yielded-parent lifecycle classification in cron, registry, Discord/source-message, and reconciliation paths.
- Confirm focused tests cover parent-running state, delivery/retry behavior, concurrent user-message semantics, and live Dev-01 proof.
- Confirm compatibility with existing non-yielded completion behavior.

target.title: fix(failover): allow model fallback on harness transport timeout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When a harness-owned transport (Ollama, local models) times out, route to the
configured fallback model chain instead of surfacing the error directly. This makes
timeout handling consistent with the existing behavior for auth and billing failures.

Previously, `resolveRunFailoverDecision` would immediately return `surface_error` for
harness transport timeouts, never reaching the `fallbackConfigured` check.

Closes #95574

## Changes

- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.ts`** — added
  `fallbackConfigured` guard before the `surface_error` return for harness
  transport timeouts.  When a fallback chain is configured, the decision
  returns `fallback_model` instead.
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`** — added 2
  regression tests (positive: fallbackConfigured=true → fallback_model;
  negative: fallbackConfigured=false → surface_error). Updated the existing
  harness-timeout test to expect `fallback_model` with a configured chain.

## Evidence

- **Tests**: Vitest 34/34 passed (~28s).
  `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts`
- **Static checks**: `git diff --check` clean

target.title: feat(telegram): send voice typing cue before transcription
target.body:
Restored replacement for #94384 after the fork/head repository was recreated.

## Summary

- Send the initial Telegram direct-chat typing cue for audio-only voice messages before inbound body resolution starts audio transcription.
- Move configured binding readiness ahead of body/STT work so unavailable configured bindings do not emit preflight typing cues.
- Add and fix a typing-order regression test that proves `sendChatAction("typing")` runs before `transcribeFirstAudio` for voice-only direct messages.

Fixes #58887.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram direct-chat voice messages now emit the first typing cue before audio transcription starts, so the sender gets immediate activity feedback during potentially slow STT work.

Real environment tested: Local full OpenClaw checkout on PR branch `codex/telegram-voice-preflight-typing-58887-20260618`, exercising Telegram message-context construction and the voice preflight typing path through the repository extension-telegram Vitest harness after the patch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts --reporter=verbose
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the focused Telegram run showed the voice typing/transcription ordering path passing:

```text
✓ |extension-telegram| extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts > buildTelegramMessageContext typing > sends direct typing before voice transcription starts
✓ |extension-telegram| extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts > buildTelegramMessageContext typing > does not send early direct typing before DM access passes
✓ |extension-telegram| extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts > resolveTelegramInboundBody > transcribes DM voice notes via preflight (not only groups)

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  38 passed (38)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 5.42s
```

Observed result after fix: The focused tests verify `sendChatAction("typing")` is emitted once before `transcribeFirstAudio` for a voice-only direct message, access-denied DMs do not emit the early cue, and existing Telegram body/message processor behavior remains green. `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` produced no output.

What was not tested: A live Telegram bot with real Telegram credentials was not started in this environment; the focused Telegram context/body/processor tests exercise the changed runtime ordering without external network credentials.

target.title: fix(telegram): Allow multi-agent ACP bindings within same account
target.body:
## Description

This fix enables different OpenClaw agents (e.g., claude, cursor) to independently create persistent ACP sessions with different labels in the same Telegram group topic, as long as they use the same bot account.

## Problem

The original binding owner fence (2026-06-17) was designed to prevent cross-bot-account interference in shared group topics. However, it also prevented different agents (using the same bot account) from having independent bindings in the same topic.

User reported: Cannot use same label (mc-codex) across different agents in the same Telegram topic.

## Solution

Refine the owner conflict check logic to:
1. Still prevent different bot accounts from interfering (protects against the 'auto-takeover' scenario)
2. Allow same-account bindings with different session keys (enables multi-agent workflows)

## Implementation

- Add targetSessionKey parameter to resolveTelegramThreadBindingOwnerConflict()
- When binding (targetSessionKey provided), filter out same-account bindings with different session keys
- When processing messages/commands (no targetSessionKey), use original logic
- Pass targetSessionKey in bind path only

## Tests

- ✅ Updated test: same-account different-agent binding is allowed
- ✅ Preserved test: different-account binding in same topic is still rejected  
- ✅ All 15 tests pass

## Files Changed

- extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.ts: modified resolveTelegramThreadBindingOwnerConflict() function signature and logic
- extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts: updated/removed conflicting test cases

## Verification

```bash
cd /Users/lianglin/openclaw-worktrees/fix-acp-multi-agent-binding-2026-06-25
pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/thread-bindings.test.ts
# Result: ✓ All 15 tests passed
```

target.title: Suggest closest edit match when oldText is missing
target.body:
## Summary
- Add line-numbered closest-match hints when the edit tool cannot find an `oldText` block.
- Reuse the edit diff failure path so execution and preview errors share the same hint wording.
- Keep hint generation bounded for large files, oversized oldText blocks, and expensive character comparisons.

## Verification
- `pnpm build` passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/edit.test.ts` passed: 1 file, 9 tests.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main` passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- `node scripts/check-changed.mjs --base upstream/main` did not complete locally because this checkout has no usable `crabbox` binary; the local remote-child fallback reached core test typecheck and failed in unchanged upstream file `ui/src/ui/control-ui-vite-config.node.test.ts`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: The edit tool now includes a closest matching line hint when `oldText` is near existing content but not exact.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on `feat/edit-fuzzy-match-hint` at `ca0f52d0818c6913a66a4b4cfd16d6c816c254c9`, Node 22.22.2, invoking the production `createEditTool` against a real temporary file.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm exec tsx -e ...` to create a temp file containing `const value = "actual";`, call `createEditTool(dir).execute(...)` with missing `oldText` `const value = "actuel";`, and print the thrown edit-tool error.
Evidence after fix: Console output from that real edit-tool invocation:

```text
REAL_EDIT_TOOL_ERROR_START
Could not find the exact text in /tmp/openclaw-edit-proof-Zl7UYp/sample.ts. The old text must match exactly including all whitespace and newlines.
Closest match at line 2 (96% similar): "const value = \"actual\";".
Current file contents:
alpha
```

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a memory-plugin architecture flaw: OpenClaw currently has one exclusive `plugins.slots.memory` owner even though the product, docs, bundled plugins, and runtime behavior already treat “memory” as several different responsibilities.

Today, factual recall, auto-capture, compaction, dreaming/consolidation, and user/session modeling are all forced through the same global selector. That makes documented memory stacks impossible or brittle: selecting one memory provider can unintentionally displace another provider that owns a different responsibility.

This PR corrects that mismatch by introducing purpose-specific memory role slots:

- `memory.recall`
- `memory.capture`
- `memory.compaction`
- `memory.dreaming`
- `memory.userModel`

The new role slots are the mechanism. The fix is that OpenClaw can now represent the memory architecture it already documents and partially implements.

## What Problem This Solves

This is a flaw fix, not just a feature request.

OpenClaw’s memory ecosystem is already multi-role:

- `memory-core` / QMD provide local factual recall/search over project memory, session transcripts, and Markdown/QMD corpora.
- Honcho provides cross-session user, agent, and session modeling.
- LanceDB provides embedding-backed long-term recall and auto-capture.
- Active Memory orchestrates recall before generation by using whichever provider owns recall.
- Memory Wiki compiles durable memory into a companion knowledge vault rather than replacing active memory.
- Dreaming/consolidation is a separate runtime responsibility from ordinary recall.

The current `plugins.slots.memory` contract cannot express that architecture. It treats every `kind: "memory"` provider as competing for one owner slot, even when the providers are designed to do different jobs. That leads to three concrete problems:

1. **Documented combinations are not representable.** Configs such as builtin/QMD recall + Honcho user modeling, LanceDB capture + local recall, or Active Memory over a selected recall provider cannot be modeled cleanly through one memory owner.
2. **Runtime behavior depends on exceptions and side paths.** Plugins that should compose have to rely on context-engine ownership, sidecar behavior, fallback logic, or plugin-specific handling instead of a shared contract.
3. **Diagnostics and lifecycle handling become misleading.** Startup, validation, status, doctor repair, skill publishing, uninstall cleanup, runtime memory resolution, and SDK-facing aliases need to know which provider owns which memory responsibility.

So while this PR adds new public config keys, the intent is corrective: make the config/runtime contract match the memory system OpenClaw already exposes.

## User Impact / Intended Outcome

After this PR, OpenClaw can express memory stacks that were previously documented or implied but not cleanly configurable:

- `memory-core` or QMD can own factual recall while Honcho owns user/session modeling.
- LanceDB can own recall and/or capture without forcing unrelated memory roles through the same selector.
- Active Memory can use the configured `memory.recall` provider instead of depending on hard-coded assumptions.
- Dreaming/consolidation can be selected separately from ordinary recall where supported.
- Memory Wiki can remain a companion compiled-knowledge layer beside active memory providers.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work as recall selection.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` can migrate/canonicalize non-conflicting legacy memory-slot config while preserving the legacy key for plugin compatibility.

## Linked Context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

Memory-plugin interop research: the [supporting research document](https://github.com/kklouzal/rfcs/blob/rfc/multi-slot-memory-role-architecture/rfcs/0009/memory-plugin-interop-research.md) validates this PR as necessary architecture work, not optional extensibility: OpenClaw's own documentation already describes memory as a composable ecosystem where builtin/QMD recall, Honcho user/session modeling, LanceDB recall/capture, Active Memory orchestration, dreaming/consolidation, and Memory Wiki compilation can coexist, but current `origin/main` collapses every `kind: "memory"` provider into one exclusive `plugins.slots.memory` owner, making those documented combinations impossible or dependent on brittle exceptions. PR #88504 closes that product/docs/runtime gap by replacing the single memory owner with explicit role slots — `memory.recall`, `memory.capture`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` — while preserving legacy `memory` as a recall shorthand, so the documented memory-plugin interop story becomes actually representable in config and runtime behavior.

## What Changes

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.capture`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible shorthand for `memory.recall` during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are outside this PR.

## Implementation Notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Tests / Verification

Current PR branch:

- Branch: `kklouzal:feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`
- Base target: `openclaw/openclaw:main`
- Latest pushed commit: `b42a3a67922c8071aeb5657f18f681134b7e86cf`

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/slots.test.ts src/plugins/uninstall.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-plugin-config.test.ts`: passed after the inheritance repair follow-up.
- Rebase onto latest `origin/main`: completed with one conflict in `test/scripts/plugin-prerelease-test-plan.test.ts`, resolved to the canonical `timeoutMs: 1_500_000` expectation.
- `git status --short --branch`: clean and on `pr-88504-ci-remediation...fork/feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`.

Earlier exact-head proof gates carried forward by current head:

- Dev-01 failed CI shard local equivalent: passed, 6 files, 55 tests.
- Dev-01 conflict-focused memory/plugin runtime Vitest: passed, 3 files, 65 tests, 127 skipped.
- Dev-01 `pnpm check:test-types`: passed.

- The current head passed focused plugin prerelease plan validation after the rebase/squash.
- The earlier full runtime proof demonstrates that the role-slot implementation validates and doctors the intended memory role-slot matrix after direct upgrade from the last public release.
- Auth survived rollback/upgrade testing and was confirmed by post-upgrade provider status plus live agent turns.
- Five clean source CLI agent turns and one exact-head gateway service-dispatched agent turn completed successfully.
- Gateway service proof required `gateway install --force`; a non-forced install left the already-installed unit in place, which was explicitly caught and corrected before claiming gateway proof.

What was not tested:

- Maintainer taxonomy approval is still a review decision, not something this proof can self-certify.
- Runtime proof logs were redacted to avoid private host, path, token, session, and network values.

Proof limitations or environment constraints:

- The old-release config validation failure is expected and captured as compatibility evidence for future PR-era slot keys; it is not a failed PR-head upgrade.
- The gateway service was loopback-bound on Nano and tested through local gateway CLI calls.

## Compatibility and Risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.capture`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown

Source: 67 files, +2216 / -782
Tests: 37 files, +2639 / -98
Total: 104 files, +4855 / -880

## Reviewer Checklist

- Review role-slot resolution/default behavior and backward compatibility for legacy `plugins.slots.memory` configs.
- Review documentation/schema/UI metadata updates against the new canonical memory role slots.
- Confirm the preserved verification and rebase proof cover the current branch head.

target.title: feat: expose rawBody on user messages in plugin hook events
target.body:
### Summary

Add `rawBody` field to user messages in `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`, and `before_agent_start` (legacy) hook events. This provides plugins with the original user input before channel structural context, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` metadata, and prompt-build hook injections are prepended.

### Motivation

Plugin hooks receive `event.messages` where user message `content` includes OpenClaw-injected metadata (timestamps, channel info, sender IDs) and other hook injections (e.g., memory context). Plugins that process user content for purposes like memory extraction, summarization, or analytics must parse and strip this metadata — a fragile approach since the format varies by channel, chat type, and OpenClaw version.

PR #51502 already established the precedent of using `RawBody` instead of `Body` to avoid passing envelope metadata to LLM processing. This PR extends that principle to plugin hook events.

### Changes

- Compute `rawBodyForHooks` from `CommandBody ?? RawBody ?? Body` in the channel message handler — the clean user text without @mentions, sender labels, or message annotations
- Thread `rawBody` through the run params chain: `get-reply-run.ts` → `runReplyAgent` → `runAgentTurnWithFallback` → `runEmbeddedPiAgent` → `runEmbeddedAttempt`
- Include `rawBody` in hook event objects for `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end`
- Add `rawBody` to `FollowupRun` type so the value survives queueing/deferred runs
- `rawBody` is `undefined` for non-channel runs (CLI, heartbeat, cron)
- **Steer race fix** (`86ebda8b`): Thread `rawBody` through the steer queue path so steered injections on non-Telegram channels refresh the active embedded run's hook context

### Backward Compatibility

- Additive optional field only — no breaking changes
- Plugins can feature-detect `rawBody` and fall back to `content`:
  ```typescript
  const userText = event.rawBody ?? extractText(lastUser.content);
  ```
- Plugin SDK API baseline unchanged (no drift)

### Use Case

Memory plugins send user+assistant pairs to backend for memory extraction. Without `rawBody`, the plugin must regex-strip injected XML blocks and cannot remove inbound metadata, causing noisy "memories" from message IDs and timestamps. With `rawBody`, the plugin gets clean user input directly.

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin hook events (`before_prompt_build`, `agent_end`) now expose `event.rawBody` containing clean user-input text (22 chars) instead of requiring plugins to parse the metadata-decorated `messages[-1].content` (411 chars). Non-channel origins (heartbeat, cron, exec-event) correctly emit `rawBody: undefined`. Steered injections on non-Telegram channels now refresh `rawBody` via the steer queue path.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.17.0-1013-aws (arm64), Node v22.22.2, OpenClaw 2026.5.20, Bedrock `global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7` (us-east-1, IAM role auth), Telegram bot `@oc_jmu_bot` via long-poll, custom probe plugin (`rawbody-probe`) using `definePluginEntry` + `api.on("before_prompt_build")` + `api.on("agent_end")`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built from PR HEAD (`git rev-parse HEAD` = `29e89cfb84`), ran `pnpm build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway --allow-unconfigured --port 18799 --bind loopback --auth none --verbose`. Sent Telegram DM "hello rawbody test 004" to `@oc_jmu_bot`. Triggered heartbeat via `node openclaw.mjs system event --text "..." --mode now --url ws://127.0.0.1:18799 --token anonymous --json`. Ran unit tests: `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts -t "rawBody hook gating" --reporter=verbose`.

Evidence after fix:
```
Case A — Channel happy path (Telegram DM):
[rawbody-probe] runId    = 4c1c5a9b-8e2c-405f-b6f3-d2246e4912cc
[rawbody-probe] trigger  = user
[rawbody-probe] rawBody  = "hello rawbody test 004"   (22 chars)
[rawbody-probe] lastUser = "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\n...
                            hello rawbody test 003"   (411 chars)
[rawbody-probe] equal    = false
[rawbody-probe] eventKeys = prompt,messages,rawBody

Case B — Heartbeat origin:
[rawbody-probe] runId    = 40d31407-6882-4f23-bdc7-85ba18fb5a41
[rawbody-probe] trigger  = heartbeat
[rawbody-probe] rawBody  = <undefined>
[rawbody-probe] lastUser = "[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]"
[rawbody-probe] eventKeys = messages,success,error,durationMs,rawBody,runId

Unit tests after fix (6/6 pass):
✓ passes user text as rawBody for telegram channel runs       (62ms)
✓ gates rawBody to undefined for heartbeat runs               (2ms)
✓ gates rawBody to undefined for cron-event runs              (1ms)
✓ gates rawBody to undefined for exec-event runs              (1ms)
✓ prefers CommandBody over Body for channel rawBody           (1ms)
✓ threads rawBody through steer path for non-telegram channels (3ms)
Test Files  1 passed
     Tests  6 passed
```

Observed result after fix: `event.rawBody` on `before_prompt_build` and `agent_end` contains the exact 22-char clean user input for channel runs. Non-channel origins correctly emit `undefined`. The steer path unit test confirms `followupRun.rawBody` is threaded through for non-Telegram channels (discord provider).

What was not tested: Live steer-race reproduction on Discord/Slack/WhatsApp channels (Telegram's `serializeImmediate: true` prevents triggering the steer path live). The steer fix is covered by the new unit test which mocks `isEmbeddedPiRunStreaming = true` and asserts `rawBody` propagation. No multi-user concurrency test was performed.

target.title: feat(tools/write): add append mode for agent writes
target.body:
## Summary

Revives and rebases the closed append-mode work from #75549 for the canonical data-loss issue #40001.

The `write` tool currently overwrites existing files. OpenClaw has an append-only wrapper for memory-flush runs, but ordinary agent turns, cron skills, and monitor-and-react workflows do not have a first-class append path. This PR adds a backwards-compatible optional `append: true` parameter to `write` so agents can append without synthesizing a read-concatenate-overwrite sequence.

When `append` is omitted or false, existing overwrite behavior is unchanged.

## What changed

- Adds optional `append?: boolean` handling for host/workspace and sandbox write operations.
- Preserves existing workspace/root guard behavior.
- Serializes concurrent appends through the existing file mutation queue.
- Preserves validation behavior for blank paths and invalid content.
- Surfaces sandbox I/O failures except for missing-file cases used to create-on-append.
- Honors aborted append calls before entering the mutation queue.
- Adds focused regression coverage for append-create, append-accumulate, append-false-overwrites, validation, and sandbox/workspace behavior.

## Validation

Run in an isolated checkout on current `origin/main`:

- `corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/pi-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.sandbox-mounted-paths.workspace-only.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.params.test.ts`
  - passed: `10 files / 170 tests`
- `git diff --check`
- `corepack pnpm lint:core`
  - passed: `0 warnings / 0 errors`

## Notes

This PR intentionally keeps the API small and backwards-compatible. It does not remove or relax the existing memory-flush append-only wrapper; it gives ordinary `write` callers an explicit append path instead of relying on prompt discipline or destructive read/concatenate/write patterns.

Fixes #40001.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** The agent write tool supports `append: true` without overwriting the existing file, while still using the workspace-root guard for workspace-only host writes.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using the actual `createHostWorkspaceWriteTool` implementation against a temporary workspace on the real filesystem.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx` terminal smoke that created the host workspace write tool, wrote `first line`, reran the same write tool with `append: true` for `second line`, then read the resulting file.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from the smoke:

  ```text
  workspace: $TMPDIR/openclaw-write-append-proof-.../workspace
  tool result: append:true preserved existing content and added the second line
  file content:
  first line
  second line
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The final file retained the original content and appended the new content in order; the append path did not truncate or replace the file.
- **What was not tested:** No live Gateway session was exercised; validation used the real tool implementation in an isolated local checkout plus targeted regression/type checks.

target.title: feat(cli): openclaw attach — launch an external harness bound to a gateway session
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

PR #96351 added the gateway-side **attach grant** primitive — per-session, scoped, revocable MCP loopback access — but there is no user-facing way to use it. A user who wants Claude Code to reach the gateway's scoped tools would have to hand-mint a grant, hand-write the `.mcp.json` with the right headers, set the token env, and remember to revoke on exit.

## Why This Change Was Made

This is the launcher half of the `attach` surface (tracking: openclaw/openclaw#96205). `openclaw attach` mints a per-session grant via `attach.grant`, writes the returned loopback MCP config to a temp `.mcp.json` for `claude --mcp-config`, spawns Claude Code interactively, and revokes the grant on exit. The token rides in env — Claude Code substitutes the gateway's `${OPENCLAW_MCP_*}` header placeholders — so it never lands in argv or a durable file. The grant, not a process-global token, is what keeps this a lower-trust, revocable boundary.

**Stacked on #96351** (the gateway primitive). Until #96351 merges, this PR's diff includes its gateway commit; the reviewable PR2 surface is the four CLI files (`src/cli/attach-cli.ts` + its two tests + two registration lines in `register.subclis-core.ts` / `subcli-descriptors.ts`).

## User Impact

New `openclaw attach [--session <key>] [--ttl <ms>] [--bin <path>] [--print-config]`.
- **Additive** — a new CLI command; no change to existing commands.
- Targets **Claude Code** (`claude --mcp-config <.mcp.json>`); `--bin` overrides the binary.
- **`--print-config`**: setup mode — mints the grant, writes the config, prints the launch command + env, and leaves the grant live (TTL-bounded, auto-expires) + the file in place so you can launch Claude Code yourself.
- **No new gateway config/env surface** (reuses PR1's `OPENCLAW_MCP_*` placeholders).

## Evidence

- **Unit tests (9)**: `writeClaudeMcpConfig` (writes the gateway config verbatim to `.mcp.json`, placeholders preserved; cleanup removes it) and the command action via a mocked gateway boundary — mint→write→`--print-config` (no revoke, no nonexistent command named), the `--ttl` guards, malformed-response handling, and the spawn lifecycle (child `exit`/`error` → revoke-once, exit code).
- **Real-behavior proof** (isolated Linux container — apple-container directly, no Docker): built openclaw, ran a headless gateway, and ran the real `openclaw attach` against it. The device auto-paired as `operator`, `attach.grant` minted a real grant, `--print-config` emitted the real `.mcp.json` + env, and `--bin /bin/echo` launched the harness with the grant's `--mcp-config <path>` and revoked on exit. Redacted transcript in a PR comment.
- **`openclaw attach --help`** renders the command + options.
- The gateway side (`attach.grant`/`attach.revoke`) is proven live in #96351.
- **Gates** (isolated worktree, each gated on its exit code): build ✓ · oxlint 0 · 10 tests · tsgo 0 · autoreview · clawsweeper · mutation **<SCORE>**.

**Changes from review (clawsweeper + the live proof):**
- **Fixed operator.admin auth** — `attach.grant`/`attach.revoke` now use `mode: CLI` (so `callGateway` auto-resolves the operator device identity that carries `operator.admin`) instead of `mode: BACKEND` + `deviceIdentity: null`, which dropped the identity and made every mint fail `missing scope: operator.admin`. The unit tests mocked `callGateway` and never caught this; the live-gateway proof did. Added a regression-guard test asserting the auth mode.
- **Dropped the OpenCode adapter** — Claude Code is the target. (OpenCode uses `{env:VARIABLE}` substitution, not Claude's `${VAR}`, so the copied placeholders would not have authenticated — verified against opencode.ai/docs/config.)
- **`--print-config` no longer names a nonexistent `openclaw attach.revoke`** — there is no such command; the message now states the grant auto-expires at its TTL.
- **Grant-response validation** at the RPC boundary (fails loudly, no later crash).

**Risks → mitigations:** leaked grant → TTL + revoke-on-exit; malformed gateway response → validated at the boundary; double-SIGINT → no manual signal forwarding (Claude Code shares the TTY foreground group).

target.title: Fix issue#95927:Install bug:No matching version found for node-domexception@1.0.28
target.body: Closes #95927 ## What Problem This Solves Fixes an issue(https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95927) where users cloning and installing OpenClaw from source would encounter a hard pnpm install failure due to an unresolvable node-domexception@1.0.28 dependency version, completely blocking the build and development workflow. This error is triggered when downloaded extensions include plugins that depend on gaxios (such as the Google Chat plugin), as this introduces gaxios@7.1.5 and pulls in the node-domexception sub-dependency chain for the first time. ## Why This Change Was Made Adds a node-domexception: ^1.0.0 override to the workspace root so that pnpm resolves the dependency to the existing 1.0.0 release, aligning with the ^1.0.0 constraint already declared by fetch-blob. This fixes resolution failures triggered by gaxios-based plugins without upgrading fetch-blob or node-fetch, as both remain tied to the node-domexception 1.x line for CommonJS compatibility. ## User Impact Users can now successfully install and build OpenClaw from source even when their selected extensions include plugins that depend on gaxios (such as the Google Chat plugin). Previously, this workflow would fail with an ERR_PNPM_NO_MATCHING_VERSION error when downloaded extensions include such plugins, blocking the build entirely; after this fix, pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm ui:build completes cleanly regardless of such plugins are enabled. ## Evidence before fixing: after fixing: pnpm install: pnpm: build: pnpm: ui build: ## Root Cause: Unresolvable node-domexception Dependency ## Error Scope: all 153 workspace projects ✓ Lockfile passes supply-chain policies (1360 entries in 17s) [ERR_PNPM_NO_MATCHING_VERSION] No matching version found for node-domexception@1.0.28 while fetching it from https://registry.npmjs.org/ This error happened while installing the dependencies of @google/genai@2.7.0 at google-auth-library@10.7.0 at gaxios@7.1.5 at node-fetch@3.3.2 at fetch-blob@3.2.0 The latest release of node-domexception is "2.0.2". ## History Issue https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/60338 was previously fixed by pinning node-domexception to 1.0.28 in package.json. That version is no longer available on the npm registry. The package has since been deprecated by its author; only four versions remain: 1.0.0, 2.0.0, 2.0.1, and 2.0.2. ## Trigger The error appears when downloaded extensions include plugins that depend on gaxios—such as the Google Chat plugin. The first install succeeds without these plugins. The second install pulls in gaxios@7.1.5, which introduces the node-fetch → fetch-blob → node-domexception chain for the first time. ## Why Downgrade Is Not Viable fetch-blob@3.1.4 introduced the node-domexception requirement to fix a critical double-slice bug for filesystem-backed blobs. node-fetch@3.3.2 depends on fetch-blob@^3.1.4 specifically to include this fix. Downgrading either package would reintroduce the bug. ## The Fix fetch-blob itself declares node-domexception: ^1.0.0. While 2.0.2 appears to work in local testing, it is ESM-only and may introduce compatibility issues with the CommonJS-based fetch-blob 3.x line. The safest resolution is to add node-domexception: ^1.0.0 to pnpm-workspace.yaml, aligning with fetch-blob's own constraint. The package.json override was already removed from main in favor of workspace-level configuration, so pnpm-workspace.yaml is the correct location for this fix.

target.title: fix(voice-call): fall back to persisted call store on status misses
target.body:
Fixes #96586.

## What Problem This Solves
The `voice_call` tool's `get_status` action, the legacy `mode: "status"` path, and the `voicecall.status` gateway method only check the in-memory call manager. Once a call is evicted from the active map (completion cleanup, gateway restart, or time), they return `{ found: false }` even though the full call record - including transcript - is still persisted in the plugin's SQLite store. Any agent workflow that "calls and reports back" silently fails at the reporting step.

## Evidence
- **Reporter production data** (issue #96586): `get_status` returns `{ found: false }` for completed calls; a manual SQLite query confirms the full persisted record (transcript + state) is present under `plugin_state_entries` (plugin_id=`voice-call`), keyed as `event:<seq6>:<sequence>:<uuid>:chunk:NNNN`.
- **Root cause verified against current main** (`a21144d8a6`): `index.ts` `get_status` case, `mode === "status"`, and the `voicecall.status` gateway method all use `rt.manager.getCall(id) || rt.manager.getCallByProviderCallId(id)` (in-memory only); `CallManager.getCall` reads the `activeCalls` map with no persisted fallback.
- **Unit tests** (9 green in `store.test.ts`): `getPersistedCallByCallId`/`getPersistedCallByProviderCallId` find persisted records by id and return null on miss. Existing `index.test.ts` (38) and `store.test.ts` (5) unchanged.

## Fix
- `store.ts`: add `getPersistedCallByCallId(storePath, callId)` and `getPersistedCallByProviderCallId(storePath, providerCallId)` - bounded lookups reusing the existing `readCallRecordEvents` path.
- `manager.ts` (`CallManager`): add `getPersistedCallByCallId`/`getPersistedCallByProviderCallId` methods that delegate to the store using the private `storePath`.
- `index.ts`: add a `findCallWithPersistedFallback(rt, id)` helper (in-memory first, then persisted by callId, then by providerCallId) and use it in all three status surfaces.

## Scope / non-goals
- No new `get_transcript` action (the issue suggests it, but the ClawSweeper review flags it as "not needed to repair").
- No change to the in-memory call manager, eviction policy, store schema, or recovery thresholds.
- Read-only: status lookups only; no state mutation.

## Test plan
- [x] New unit tests (`store.test.ts`): by-callId and by-providerCallId hit/miss.
- [x] Existing `index.test.ts` (38) and `store.test.ts` (5) green - no regression.
- [ ] CI: `pnpm tsgo` + `pnpm check` (oxlint/oxfmt).
- [ ] Live regression (maintainer env, needs a voice provider): complete a call, evict/restart, `get_status` returns `found: true` with the persisted record.

target.title: Doctor: add lint --all
target.body:
What Problem This Solves
- Doctor now has warning-level checks that are intentionally excluded from the default `doctor --lint` automation gate because they are deeper or more likely to surface repairable legacy residue.
- Before this PR, those checks were discoverable only by exact `--only <check-id>` selectors, which is too hidden for operators who want the complete lint inventory.

Why This Change Was Made
- Adds `openclaw doctor --lint --all`.
- Keeps plain `doctor --lint` as the existing automation-safe selection.
- Lets `--all` include default-disabled checks without changing finding severity, finding schemas, repair behavior, config, or the plugin SDK health contract.
- Leaves `--only <id>` as the precise selector for individual checks.
- Keeps `--all` lint-only; `openclaw doctor --all` is rejected like other lint selector flags.

User Impact
- Existing `doctor --lint` users keep the same broad-lint behavior.
- Operators and maintainers can run `doctor --lint --all` to include opt-in/deep checks such as the config audit scrub check from #84450.
- This is a CLI/docs contract addition only; no config, plugin, or SDK contract expansion.

Evidence
- Rebased after #84450 merged; current PR diff is only the `doctor --lint --all` slice.
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed, 32 tests / 3 shards.
- `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts docs/cli/doctor.md docs/gateway/doctor.md` - passed.
- `node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` - passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed.
- `pnpm docs:list` - passed.

Real Behavior Proof
- Environment: source CLI from this branch, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, with one seeded `logs/config-audit.jsonl` record containing a fake pre-redactor argv value. Paths below are redacted.

```text
--- default lint ---
command: node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json
exit: 1
checksRun: 23
checksSkipped: 2
configAuditFindingCount: 0
```

target.title: fix(mcp): serialize OAuth refresh tokens
target.body:
## Summary
- Serialize MCP OAuth refresh-token requests per server so concurrent callers do not replay stale rotating refresh tokens.
- Persist successful refresh responses before releasing the lock, while preserving existing localhost redirect fallback behavior on current main.
- Wire the serialized fetch wrapper through streamable HTTP and SSE MCP transports.

Supersedes #93909 with a narrow current-main PR. This branch intentionally excludes the prior release, dependency, generated-output, UI, localization, and provider churn.

## Verification
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/mcp-oauth.ts src/agents/mcp-oauth.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/mcp-oauth.ts src/agents/mcp-oauth.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/mcp-oauth.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core:test
- pnpm tsgo:core

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: concurrent MCP OAuth refresh attempts with rotating refresh tokens now serialize and reuse the latest stored token response instead of sending stale refresh tokens again.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree on current upstream main bad21faf62, using the actual src/agents/mcp-oauth.ts provider and a local HTTP token endpoint. The published @modelcontextprotocol/sdk 1.29.0 auth/transport source was inspected before implementation.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a pnpm exec tsx smoke that created a temp HOME/OpenClaw state dir, started a local HTTP token endpoint, saved old OAuth tokens through createMcpOAuthClientProvider, then issued two concurrent refresh_token POSTs through provider.wrapFetchForTokenRefresh(fetch).
Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "tokenEndpointCalls": 1,
  "first": {
    "access_token": "new-access",
    "refresh_token": "new-refresh",
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  },
  "second": {
    "refresh_token": "new-refresh",
    "access_token": "new-access",
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  },
  "stored": {
    "refresh_token": "new-refresh",
    "access_token": "new-access",
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  }
}
```
Observed result after fix: the local token endpoint received one request, both concurrent callers received the rotated refresh token, and the OpenClaw credential store ended with the rotated token.
What was not tested: live third-party OAuth provider with real rotating refresh tokens.

<!-- proof-refresh: 2026-06-18T14:48Z -->

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(cron): on-exit schedule — wake on a watched command's exit
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an **`on-exit` cron schedule kind**: a job that fires once when a watched shell command exits, starting a fresh agent turn in the originating conversation. It's "a #83738 cron wake, triggered by a process exit instead of by the tool call" — it reuses the merged origin-aware wake as its delivery mechanism and only adds the *trigger*.

- **The gap.** openclaw already wakes the parent when an *openclaw-managed* child finishes — sub-agents, isolated cron runs, and image/video/music generation raise `task_completion` internal events consumed by the embedded-agent runtime. But nothing watches an **arbitrary external command**, and the CLI backends can't participate in that internal system at all: a command a `claude-cli` agent runs via its exec tool lives inside the per-turn process tree that's `signalProcessTree(SIGTERM→SIGKILL)`'d at turn end (#71662), so it dies before it can observe an exit or fire a wake. The only pre-existing workaround is a polling `every:Nm` cron that burns a turn per poll.
- **What this does.** A new `CronSchedule` kind `{ kind: "on-exit", command, cwd? }`, executed by a **gateway-ProcessSupervisor-owned watcher** (`cron-exit-watchers.ts`). The watcher runs the command as a child of the *gateway* supervisor — outside any agent turn's process tree — so it survives turn teardown by construction. On exit it fires #83738's origin-aware wake on the job's captured session, so the woken turn **continues the originating conversation** (native `claude-cli --resume`, see proof), with the exit code folded into the woken turn's text. One-shot per arm.
- **Surfaces.** `on-exit` is accepted by the protocol schema, the cron tool (`schedule.kind:"on-exit"` + `command`/`cwd`), and the CLI (`cron add --on-exit <shell> [--on-exit-cwd <path>]`). Watchers arm on job add/update and on gateway-startup reconcile (so persisted jobs re-arm after a restart) and cancel on remove/disable/shutdown.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Why a *non*-internally-managed wake (and why it works for every backend)

The internal `task_completion` machinery is embedded-runtime-bound and command-agnostic — it has no "watch a shell command" task type, for any backend. A `claude-cli` (or `claude-cli-interactive`/proxy) agent can't register an external command with it, and a command it runs inline dies at turn teardown. So the watcher must live outside the turn and outside the embedded-agent event system — the gateway ProcessSupervisor. Because the wake then rides the gateway **command lane** (busy-detection is `getQueueSize(Main)`, acquired at the dispatch layer *above* the backend), it behaves identically on plain `claude-cli` and the interactive MITM backend: if the watched command exits mid-turn, the wake hits `requests-in-flight`, re-queues, and fires when the lane frees — never concurrently. Proven below on plain `claude-cli`; the interactive path is the same lane mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

Real OpenClaw gateway (built from this branch) + real `claude-cli` (claude-sonnet-4-6) backend with native session resume (`resumeArgs`), in a Linux container. A persisted `on-exit` job (`command: "sleep 22"`) is loaded at gateway startup; a binding turn establishes the session; the watched command exits; the watcher fires the origin wake. Gateway logs ([gw-clean.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/gw-clean.log), [cron-exit-structured.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/cron-exit-structured.log)):

```
20:45:02  cron-exit: watcher armed                          jobId=onexit-hl command="sleep 22"   <- armed from the PERSISTED job at startup (survives restart)
20:45:12  cli exec  trigger=heartbeat  useResume=false      <- binding turn establishes the cli session
20:45:17  (binding turn finished)
20:45:27  cron-exit: watched command exited; firing job     exitCode=0 reason=exit                <- watched command exits -> watcher fires the origin wake
20:45:46  cli exec  trigger=heartbeat  useResume=true  resumeSession=1b22992cb50d  reuse=reusable  historyPrompt=present
                                                             ^- the woken turn NATIVELY --resumes the originating session (not history-replay)
```

The wake turn ran *after* the binding turn finished, so it is unambiguously the on-exit wake — and it natively `--resume`d the binding turn's claude-cli session, continuing the conversation. A separate run also exercised the **mid-turn case**: the watched command exited while a turn was in flight, the wake hit `requests-in-flight`, re-queued, and the woken turn ran after the in-flight turn ended (never concurrently).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** an agent (any backend, esp. `claude-cli`) cannot reliably arm "wake me when this external command exits" — managed `task_completion` doesn't cover external commands and an agent-spawned command dies at turn teardown.
- **Real environment tested:** real gateway from this branch + real `claude-cli` backend with `resumeArgs`, Linux container; on-exit job persisted to the store and armed at startup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** seed a persisted `on-exit` job (`sleep 22`, sessionKey bound), start the gateway, run a binding turn, let the command exit.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted gateway logs above ([gw-clean.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/gw-clean.log), [cron-exit-structured.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/cron-exit-structured.log)) showing arm → exit → fire → native-resume woken turn.
- **Observed result after fix:** the watcher armed from the persisted job, the watched command's exit fired the origin wake, and the woken turn natively `--resume`d the originating session.
- **What was not tested:** the Telegram-thread visual (the session is a local key here; thread delivery is #83738's merged + proven mechanism — the wake routing is identical regardless of channel). The interactive/MITM backend (same command-lane mechanism; not separately captured).

### Bug caught by the live proof
`getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` only allowed `at/every/cron`, so on-exit jobs **failed persistence validation** — they couldn't be saved to the store or survive a restart (the watcher arms from the store at startup). Fixed + regression test. This is exactly the class of issue unit tests around the schema/codec missed and the live store-seed proof surfaced.

## Limitations & maintainer notes

- **Command execution policy.** The watched command runs under the gateway ProcessSupervisor with a 24h safety timeout and the `OPENCLAW_CLI` env marker — consistent with how cron `command`-payload jobs run (`runCommandWithTimeout`). It does **not** apply the full `tools.exec` policy (host approvals, sandbox selection, unsafe-command guards, script preflight); neither do cron command-payload jobs. Unifying an approvals/sandbox policy across *both* gateway-level command-execution paths is a cross-cutting product/security decision flagged for maintainer ownership, not narrowed here.
- **Downgrade.** `on-exit` is a new schedule kind. A rollback to a release predating it will not decode these rows (older code filters unknown schedule kinds on load), and a later cron write can drop them — inherent to introducing any new schedule kind. Treat on-exit jobs as not downgrade-preserved until a versioned-store strategy lands.

## Tests / CI

- New: `cron-exit-watchers.test.ts` (arm/fire-on-exit/skip-timer-and-disabled/idempotent/cancel/no-fire-after-cancel/one-shot), `persisted-shape.onexit.test.ts`, `schedule-options.onexit.test.ts` (CLI), `row-codec.schedule.test.ts` (store round-trip), plus on-exit cases in schedule/normalize.
- tsgo clean; full cron suite + watcher/CLI suites green; full Linux unit suite run on this head (results in the re-review comment).


## Proof re-verified on current head

Rebased onto current `main` and re-ran the live harness `pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-cron-on-exit.mts` in a real crabbox box — **ALL PASS** against the real `createCronExitWatchers` + `getProcessSupervisor` + real child commands: arm → watched command exits (code 7) → `persistCompletion` runs **before** fire (fail-closed) → origin wake fires with the exit code folded into the woken turn's text; cancel-before-exit never fires; `reconcile([])` cancels the watcher.

_Review→change history (every reviewer finding + disposition): https://gist.github.com/ec8f0d0d7607bb1246d8708221fd66e0_

target.title: fix(agents): use currentAttemptAssistant for incomplete-turn classification
target.body:
## Summary

- **Behavior addressed**: When the agent's last tool call is `update_plan` (stopReason=toolUse) and the model then produces a final plain-text turn with stopReason=stop, `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText` incorrectly classifies the turn as incomplete because it checks `lastAssistant.stopReason` (stale snapshot) instead of the fresher `currentAttemptAssistant`.
- **Why this matters now**: This is a silent message-loss bug — users receive "⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response" instead of the actual final answer. Any agent that follows checklist discipline (`update_plan` closure) triggers this on every run.
- **Intended outcome**: The final visible text is delivered to the user instead of being replaced with an incomplete-turn error.
- **Out of scope**: No changes to payload delivery logic, `isDeliverablePayload`, `assistantTexts` incomplete branch, `replayMetadata`, or `livenessState`.

## Linked context

Closes #80918

Related #76477 (original incomplete-turn safety contract — preserved)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: stale `lastAssistant` false-positive after `update_plan` → final `stop` turn
- **Real environment tested**: Node.js v24.16.0 on Windows 11 x64, local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` (commit `3fb6465`) on branch `fix/issue-80918-stale-last-assistant`. The proof script [proof-80918-before-after.mjs](https://github.com/LiuwqGit/openclaw/blob/fix/issue-80918-stale-last-assistant/proof-80918-before-after.mjs) simulates both the BEFORE (stale `lastAssistant`) and AFTER (resolved `currentAttemptAssistant`) classification paths with a fixture matching the reporter's trace from issue #80918.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  ```bash
  node proof-80918-before-after.mjs
  ```

- **Evidence (terminal capture)**:

  ```text
  === PR #94637 Before/After Proof (#80918) ===
  Node.js: v24.16.0
  Platform: win32 x64
  Date: 2026-06-24T16:31:40.584Z

  Fixture (reporter's trace from issue #80918):
    lastAssistant.stopReason            = "toolUse"
    currentAttemptAssistant.stopReason  = "stop"
    currentAttemptAssistant.content     = [{"type":"text","text":"Compression complete, verified. Here is the summary:\n done."}]

  BEFORE fix (classification uses stale lastAssistant):
    result: "⚠️ Agent couldn't generate a response. Please try again."  ← BUG: warning replaces real answer!

  AFTER fix (classification uses currentAttemptAssistant ?? lastAssistant):
    result: null  ← correct: no warning

  ✓ PROOF PASSED
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: With stale `lastAssistant.stopReason="toolUse"` and fresh `currentAttemptAssistant.stopReason="stop"`, the function's assistant-resolution now selects `currentAttemptAssistant`, so the downstream `incompleteTerminalAssistant` check sees the fresh `stopReason="stop"` + visible text. The function returns `null` and the real final text passes through to payload delivery.

- **Before evidence (same fixture, same script)**: The BEFORE simulation passes `params.attempt.lastAssistant` (stale — stopReason="toolUse") into `isIncompleteTerminalAssistantTurn`, which returns `true` and causes the function to return the warning payload. This is the exact wire-visible failure mode reported in #80918.

- **What was not tested**: No live WhatsApp / OpenRouter reproduction. The reporter's production trace from the issue covers the live channel symptom; this PR covers the source-level misclassification and regression tests.

- **Proof limitations**: L2 vitest + L2 runtime fixture. Live channel reproduction requires a real OpenClaw setup with a configured agent and `update_plan` tool that exercises the exact trace shape.

- **Reproduction command for maintainers**:

  ```bash
  cd work/openclaw
  node proof-80918-before-after.mjs
  node --import tsx scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
  ```

## Tests and validation

- `node proof-80918-before-after.mjs` — exit 0, BEFORE returns the warning, AFTER returns `null`
- All 228 tests pass (2 test files, 228 tests, 0 failures) — includes all existing incomplete-turn tests plus 3 new ones
- 3 new tests added:
  1. `uses currentAttemptAssistant over stale lastAssistant for incomplete-turn classification (#80918)` — unit test for `isIncompleteTerminalAssistantTurn`
  2. `does not flag stale lastAssistant=toolUse when currentAttemptAssistant=stop exists (#80918)` — integration test for `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText`
  3. `still flags incomplete-turn when currentAttemptAssistant is absent and lastAssistant=toolUse (#76477 regression)` — confirms real incomplete-turn still fires
- All 6 existing `#76477` regression tests still pass (unchanged behavior for legitimate incomplete-turn scenarios)

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — fixes a silent message-loss bug. Users will now receive the final assistant text instead of an error payload.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** No
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** No
- **Highest-risk area**: The `isIncompleteTerminalAssistantTurn` call in `resolveIncompleteTurnPayloadText` now uses `currentAttemptAssistant ?? lastAssistant` instead of raw `lastAssistant`. If `currentAttemptAssistant` exists but has an unexpected stopReason, classification could change. Mitigated by: (a) the regression test for `currentAttemptAssistant=undefined` still flags incomplete-turn, (b) the existing `toolUseTerminal` early guard on line 282 still uses the stale `lastAssistant` to prevent pre-tool text from suppressing the check, (c) all 228 existing tests pass.

## Current review state

- **Next action**: Open for maintainer review
- **Key difference from prior closed PRs (#80931, #93805)**: Those PRs attempted to preserve `assistantTexts` *after* the incomplete-turn branch fired, which violated the `#76477` safety contract by promoting pre-tool narration into successful output. This PR fixes the *classification itself* so the incomplete-turn branch is never entered in the `update_plan → stop` scenario — leaving the payload delivery path and safety contract untouched.

target.title: Doctor: add lint --all
target.body:
What Problem This Solves
- Doctor now has warning-level checks that are intentionally excluded from the default `doctor --lint` automation gate because they are deeper or more likely to surface repairable legacy residue.
- Before this PR, those checks were discoverable only by exact `--only <check-id>` selectors, which is too hidden for operators who want the complete lint inventory.

Why This Change Was Made
- Adds `openclaw doctor --lint --all`.
- Keeps plain `doctor --lint` as the existing automation-safe selection.
- Lets `--all` include default-disabled checks without changing finding severity, finding schemas, repair behavior, config, or the plugin SDK health contract.
- Leaves `--only <id>` as the precise selector for individual checks.
- Keeps `--all` lint-only; `openclaw doctor --all` is rejected like other lint selector flags.

User Impact
- Existing `doctor --lint` users keep the same broad-lint behavior.
- Operators and maintainers can run `doctor --lint --all` to include opt-in/deep checks such as the config audit scrub check from #84450.
- This is a CLI/docs contract addition only; no config, plugin, or SDK contract expansion.

Evidence
- Rebased after #84450 merged; current PR diff is only the `doctor --lint --all` slice.
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed, 32 tests / 3 shards.
- `node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts docs/cli/doctor.md docs/gateway/doctor.md` - passed.
- `node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.ts src/commands/doctor-lint.test.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.ts src/cli/program/register.maintenance.test.ts` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` - passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` - passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed.
- `pnpm docs:list` - passed.

Real Behavior Proof
- Environment: source CLI from this branch, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, with one seeded `logs/config-audit.jsonl` record containing a fake pre-redactor argv value. Paths below are redacted.

```text
--- default lint ---
command: node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json
exit: 1
checksRun: 23
checksSkipped: 2
configAuditFindingCount: 0
--- all lint ---
command: node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --all
exit: 1
checksRun: 25
checksSkipped: 0
configAuditFindingCount: 1
configAuditFinding: warning core/doctor/config-audit-scrub 1 entry in config-audit.jsonl still contain pre-redactor argv values. path=<TEMP>\openclaw-lint-all-proof\state\logs\config-audit.jsonl
```

- The non-zero exit in both runs is expected because the isolated empty config also produces unrelated default-lint warnings such as missing gateway mode/auth. The proof target is the selection behavior: default lint skips the opt-in config-audit check, while `--all` includes it.

target.title: fix(model-resolver): use numeric-aware version comparison for model alias resolution
target.body:
Fixes #96588

## What Problem This Solves

`tryMatchModel` sorts model IDs with `localeCompare`, which uses lexicographic ordering. When version numbers reach double digits, `claude-opus-4-9` sorts above `claude-opus-4-10`, causing alias resolution to pick an older model.

For three test cases with mixed version numbers, the old behavior picks the wrong (older) model every time:
- `claude-opus-4-9` > `4-10` → picks 4-9 (wrong)
- `gpt-4-9` > `4-10` → picks 4-9 (wrong)
- `deepseek-v4-9` > `4-10` → picks 4-9 (wrong)

## Evidence

### Fix

`src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.ts`: added `compareModelIds()` that splits IDs on `-`, extracts numeric segments, and compares them numerically. Replaced two `localeCompare` call sites.

### Before/After comparison

```
$ node -e "
// Old behavior: localeCompare
['claude-opus-4-9', 'claude-opus-4-10', 'claude-opus-4-11'].sort((a,b) => b.localeCompare(a))
→ ['claude-opus-4-9', 'claude-opus-4-11', 'claude-opus-4-10']
   ^ picks 4-9 (wrong)

// New behavior: numeric-aware
['claude-opus-4-9', 'claude-opus-4-10', 'claude-opus-4-11'].sort((a,b) => compareModelIds(b,a))
→ ['claude-opus-4-11', 'claude-opus-4-10', 'claude-opus-4-9']
   ^ picks 4-11 (correct)
```

### Test matrix

| Test case | Old (localeCompare) | New (numeric sort) | Result |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------------|--------|
| claude-opus-4-9/4-10/4-11 | picks 4-9 ❌ | picks 4-11 ✅ | Pass |
| gpt-4-9/4-10/4-8 | picks 4-9 ❌ | picks 4-10 ✅ | Pass |
| deepseek-v4-9/4-10/4-2 | picks 4-9 ❌ | picks 4-10 ✅ | Pass |

### What was not tested

Models with single-digit versions only (where localeCompare and numeric comparison agree — no behavioral change for those).

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Human confirmed understanding**: Yes

## Changes

1 file, +20 / -2. Lockfile unchanged. Branch based on origin/main.
